steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find one dimension from given info
steps.content:
The slant $AB = 84$, the height $h = 42$, and the radius $r$ form a right triangle (apex $A$, center of base, point $B$ on the rim). Apply Pythagoras:
$$r^2 = AB^2 - h^2 = 84^2 - 42^2 = 7056 - 1764 = 5292$$

steps.title: Compute the requested value
steps.content:
Use the cone-volume formula $V = \tfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$:
$$V = \tfrac{1}{3}\pi(5292)(42) = \pi(1764)(42) = 74088\pi$$
So $k = 74088$. The answer is 74088.
